sections.paragraph:
Ἁλφαῖος, ου, ὁ (Ἀ other edd.; Hebr. חַלְפִּי, cp. Syr.; MLidzbarski, Handb. d. nordsem. Epigraphik 1898, 275; TNöldeke, Beiträge z. semit. Sprachwissenschaft 1904, 98; Procop. Gaz., Ep. 99) Alphaeus.

① father of Levi the tax-collector Λευὶν τὸν τοῦ Ἁ. Mk 2:14; Lk 5:27 D; GPt 14:60.

② father of one of the 12 disciples, who is called Ἰάκωβος ὁ τοῦ Ἁ. to distinguish him from the son of Zebedee Mt 10:3; Mk 3:18, also simply Ἰ. Ἁλφαίου Lk 6:15; Ac 1:13.—On him, and the attempts to equate 2 w. Clopas, as well as 1 w. 2, s. Zahn, Forsch. 6, 1900, 323f; JChapman, JTS 7, 1906, 412ff; FMaier, BZ 4, 1906, 164ff; 255ff.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

BZ BZ = Biblische Zeitschrift—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 48
